tools.reason: Explicit registry: skills tool with controlled write or workflow side effects.

tools.name: unreal.spawn_actor
tools.title: Spawn Actor
tools.description: Spawns an actor from a class path with optional transform and freeform property overrides; legacy flexible path for expert use.
tools.reason: Explicit registry: actors tool with controlled write or workflow side effects.
tools.notes: Legacy spawn tool supports freeform property overrides; prefer unreal.spawn_actor_basic or unreal.spawn_static_mesh_actor.

tools.name: unreal.spawn_actor_basic
tools.title: Spawn Actor (Basic)
tools.description: Spawns a fixed-schema actor at the requested transform; safer than the legacy freeform unreal.spawn_actor path.
tools.reason: Explicit registry: actors tool with controlled write or workflow side effects.
tools.notes: AI-facing fixed-schema wrapper routed to the legacy handler.

tools.name: unreal.spawn_actor_batch
tools.title: Spawn Actor Batch
tools.description: Spawns multiple actors from class paths with optional transforms and property overrides; legacy flexible batch path.
tools.reason: Explicit registry: actors tool with controlled write or workflow side effects.
tools.notes: Legacy batch spawn supports freeform item objects; prefer unreal.spawn_actor_batch_basic.

tools.name: unreal.spawn_actor_batch_basic
tools.title: Spawn Actor Batch (Basic)
tools.description: Spawns multiple fixed-schema actors in one call using class paths, labels, and transforms without freeform property maps.
tools.reason: Explicit registry: actors tool with controlled write or workflow side effects.
tools.notes: AI-facing fixed-schema wrapper routed to the legacy handler.

tools.name: unreal.spawn_static_mesh_actor
tools.title: Spawn Static Mesh Actor
tools.description: Spawns a StaticMeshActor with a mesh asset, transform, label, and optional material assignment in the current level.
tools.reason: Explicit registry: actors tool with controlled write or workflow side effects.

tools.name: unreal.start_pie
tools.title: Start PIE
tools.description: Starts Play-In-Editor for the current map and returns the resulting PIE state.
tools.reason: Explicit registry: low-risk editor session operation.

tools.name: unreal.stop_pie
tools.title: Stop PIE
tools.description: Stops any active Play-In-Editor session and returns the resulting editor state.
tools.reason: Explicit registry: low-risk editor session operation.

tools.name: unreal.sync_content_browser
tools.title: Sync Content Browser
tools.description: Selects and focuses one or more assets in the Content Browser without changing their contents.
tools.reason: Explicit registry: low-risk editor session operation.

tools.name: unreal.tail_log
tools.title: Tail Editor Log
tools.description: Reads the tail of the current Unreal Editor log for recent warnings, errors, and MCP diagnostics.
tools.reason: Explicit registry: read-only inspection, audit, status, schema validation, memory read, or skill read tool.

tools.name: unreal.verify_task_outcome
tools.title: Verify Task Outcome
tools.description: Checks task completion evidence using tools/list, snapshot diffs, and required text evidence.
tools.reason: Explicit registry: read-only final task verification based on tools/list, snapshots, and evidence text.

tools.name: unreal.widget_add
tools.title: Add Widget
tools.description: Adds a widget to a Widget Blueprint tree under the requested parent and marks the asset dirty.
tools.reason: Explicit registry: Widget write tool with automated disposable sandbox category coverage.

tools.name: unreal.widget_bind_blueprint_variable
tools.title: Bind Widget Variable
tools.description: Binds a Widget Blueprint variable to a named widget so graph and designer code can reference it.
tools.reason: Explicit registry: widget tool with controlled write or workflow side effects.

tools.name: unreal.widget_bind_event
tools.title: Bind Widget Event
tools.description: Binds a widget event to a generated Blueprint graph handler and reports the created binding evidence.
tools.reason: Explicit registry: widget tool with controlled write or workflow side effects.

tools.name: unreal.widget_build_template
tools.title: Build Widget Template
tools.description: Builds a predefined Widget Blueprint layout template for common HUD or panel workflows.
tools.reason: Explicit registry: Widget template write tool with automated disposable sandbox category coverage.

tools.name: unreal.widget_dump_tree
tools.title: Dump Widget Tree
tools.description: Read-only inspection of a Widget Blueprint tree, widget variables, slots, and optional EventGraph nodes.
tools.reason: Explicit registry: Widget tree inspection tool for AI self-verification before and after UMG edits.

tools.name: unreal.widget_remove
tools.title: Remove Widget
tools.description: Removes a named widget from a Widget Blueprint tree and marks the asset dirty.
tools.reason: Explicit registry: widget tool with controlled write or workflow side effects.

tools.name: unreal.widget_set_property
tools.title: Set Widget Property
tools.description: Sets a supported property on a named widget inside a Widget Blueprint.
tools.reason: Explicit registry: widget tool with controlled write or workflow side effects.

tools.name: unreal.widget_set_slot_layout
tools.title: Set Widget Slot Layout
tools.description: Updates slot layout fields such as anchors, offsets, alignment, or size for a widget with a parent slot.
tools.reason: Explicit registry: widget tool with controlled write or workflow side effects.

tools.name: unreal.widget_rename
tools.title: Rename Widget
tools.description: Renames a widget node inside a Widget Blueprint, preserving bindings, variable references, animation bindings, and designer hierarchy references. Use this to rename a button, text block, panel, or other UMG widget by name.
tools.reason: UMG parity tool: rename a button widget, text block, panel, image, or designer widget while preserving bindings and Blueprint variable references.
tools.notes: Binding preservation is covered by C++ automation helper tests and Widget fixture notes; graph/event reference coverage uses existing widget_dump_tree readback.

tools.name: unreal.widget_reorder_child
tools.title: Reorder Widget
tools.description: Moves a widget to a new sibling index inside its parent panel in a Widget Blueprint. Use this when a user asks to move a button, text block, image, or panel earlier or later in a UMG layout.
tools.reason: UMG parity tool: reorder children inside a Canvas, VerticalBox, HorizontalBox, Overlay, or other panel by widget name.
tools.notes: Low-risk hierarchy edit; root widgets without a parent panel are refused.

tools.name: unreal.widget_duplicate
tools.title: Duplicate Widget
tools.description: Duplicates a widget node inside the same Widget Blueprint, optionally copying its child subtree and assigning unique names. Use this when duplicating a UMG button, text block, card, panel, or layout group.
tools.reason: UMG parity tool: duplicate a button, text block, panel, or complete widget subtree with collision-safe generated names.
tools.notes: The deep binding-integrity fixture is deferred to C++ helper coverage plus widget_dump_tree readback because JSON fixtures cannot construct arbitrary UMG bindings by themselves.

tools.name: unreal.widget_delete
tools.title: Delete Widget
tools.description: Deletes a widget subtree from a Widget Blueprint after checking bindings, bound events, and animation references. Use force only when the user explicitly wants to remove referenced UMG widgets.
tools.reason: UMG parity tool: safely delete a button, text block, panel, or widget subtree and refuse when bindings would break unless force=true.
tools.notes: Mirrors Blueprint delete discipline: force=false refuses referenced widgets instead of silently breaking UMG bindings.

tools.name: unreal.material_instance_list
tools.title: List Material Instances
tools.description: Lists Material Instance assets under a content path with parent material and class metadata. Use this first when a user asks to edit a mesh material, make a material redder, or find editable material instances.
tools.reason: Material discovery tool for workflows like list material instances, inspect parameters, then set scalar or vector color/tint values.
tools.notes: Phase 1.5 ships list/get/set_scalar/set_vector. Texture and static switch setters are deferred for asset-reference and permutation safety.

tools.name: unreal.material_instance_get_parameters
tools.title: Get Material Parameters
tools.description: Reads scalar, vector, texture, and static switch parameters from a Material Instance. Use this before changing brightness, roughness, color, tint, emissive color, or other material parameters.
tools.reason: Material inspection tool for choosing whether to set a scalar value such as brightness or a vector value such as BaseColor/Tint to make a material redder.
tools.notes: Read-only. Returns fromParent=true when a value is inherited and includeInherited=true.

tools.name: unreal.material_instance_set_scalar
tools.title: Set Material Scalar
tools.description: Sets a scalar parameter on a Material Instance Constant with strict numeric input and previous-value readback. Use this for brightness, roughness, metallic, opacity, intensity, or other numeric material edits.
tools.reason: Type-safe scalar setter for material instance parameter changes; rejects value strings with errorKind TypeMismatch instead of coercing.
tools.notes: Does not set texture or static switch parameters; those are future v0.16.x work because they need asset-reference and permutation invalidation discipline.

tools.name: unreal.material_instance_set_vector
tools.title: Set Material Vector
tools.description: Sets an RGBA vector parameter on a Material Instance Constant with strict object input and previous-value readback. Use this for color, tint, emissive color, or make this material redder requests.
tools.reason: Type-safe vector setter for material colors and tints; useful when the user says make this material redder, bluer, brighter, or change its emissive color.
tools.notes: Does not set texture or static switch parameters; those are future v0.16.x work because they need asset-reference and permutation invalidation discipline.

tools.name: unreal.chat_label_active_task
tools.title: Label Active Chat Task
tools.description: Sets or clears a user-visible label for subsequent launch-session ActivityLog events.
tools.reason: Explicit registry: low-risk launch-session label setter for ActivityLog task labels.

tools.name: unreal.editor.engine_version
tools.title: Editor Engine Version
tools.description: Returns the running Unreal Editor engine version as structured major, minor, patch, and version_string fields.
tools.reason: Explicit registry: read-only editor engine version inspection for version-aware tool planning.

tools.name: unreal.editor.python_runtime_info
tools.title: Editor Python Runtime Info
tools.description: Reports read-only Python runtime interpreter, module-count, and Unreal module details to confirm the python implementation track is working.
tools.reason: Python implementation track smoke tool. Underlying operation is read-only, but Lane E defaults python-track registry entries to critical risk and external-process handling.
tools.summaryTemplate: Reported Unreal Python {result.python.versionInfo.major}.{result.python.versionInfo.minor} with {result.python.moduleCount} loaded modules

policy.reason: Python implementation track smoke tool. Underlying operation is read-only, but Lane E defaults python-track registry entries to critical risk and external-process handling.
policy.summaryTemplate: Reported Unreal Python {result.python.versionInfo.major}.{result.python.versionInfo.minor} with {result.python.moduleCount} loaded modules

tools.notes: Read-only canonical first Python-track smoke tool; use to verify PythonScriptPlugin bridge execution and handler hash enforcement.

tools.name: unreal.actor_get_property
tools.title: Get Actor Property
tools.description: Reads a single UProperty value from a named actor, including supported dot-paths through struct or object properties.
tools.reason: v0.15 chunk 1 C++ readback inspector: read-only actor property access closes the get/inspect gap without Python.

actorPath.description: Full actor label, actor name, or unique actor path to inspect.

propertyName.description: UProperty name or dot-path, for example bHidden, Tags, or StaticMeshComponent.StaticMesh.

tools.notes: C++ only. Supports dot notation through reflected struct and object properties; unsupported values fall back to exported text where possible.

tools.name: unreal.actor_get_transform
tools.title: Get Actor Transform
tools.description: Reads an actor transform in world or relative space without mutating editor state.
tools.reason: v0.15 chunk 1 C++ readback inspector: read-only actor transform access closes the get/inspect gap without Python.

actorPath.description: Full actor label, actor name, or unique actor path to inspect.

tools.notes: C++ only. Reads during PIE because it does not mutate editor or world state.

tools.name: unreal.automation_list
tools.title: List Automation Tests
tools.description: Lists runnable Unreal Automation Framework tests with canonical fullName, display name, flags, and optional details.
tools.reason: v0.20 C1a verification foundation read-only automation test discovery.

filter.description: Optional case-insensitive substring filter matched against fullName and displayName.

tools.notes: Uses FAutomationTestFramework::GetValidTestNames. includeDetails adds category and best-effort requirement hints.

tools.name: unreal.automation_run
tools.title: Run Automation Test
tools.description: Queues one Unreal Automation Framework test and returns immediately with a runId for polling.
tools.reason: v0.20 C1a verification foundation async single automation test runner with state-file reports.

tags.description: Caller metadata stored in the report, not passed to UE Automation filters.

tools.notes: Only one queued/running automation run is accepted at a time. tags are caller metadata only.

tools.name: unreal.automation_report
tools.title: Get Automation Run Report
tools.description: Returns the current or persisted report for an async automation test run.
tools.reason: v0.20 C1a verification foundation read-only automation run polling/report tool.
tools.notes: The public report shape excludes internal state-file bookkeeping such as timeoutSecondsConfigured.

tools.name: unreal.pie_smoke
tools.title: Run PIE Smoke
tools.description: Queues a Play In Editor smoke verification run, observes BeginPIE/alive-window/EndPIE, and returns a runId for automation_report polling.
tools.reason: v0.22 C1b PIE runtime smoke verification with shared automation_report polling.

mapPath.description: Optional /Game/... UWorld asset path. If provided, the editor opens it before PIE when the current map differs and is not dirty.

timeoutSeconds.description: Total wall-clock timeout. Values are clamped to 10..300.

aliveWindowSeconds.description: PIE alive-window duration. Values are clamped to 1..30 and must be less than timeoutSeconds.

tools.notes: Shares the AutomationRuns state-file namespace and active-run lock with automation_run; poll with unreal.automation_report.

tools.name: unreal.editor_diagnostics
tools.title: Read Editor Diagnostics
tools.description: Returns recent warning, error, and fatal Output Log diagnostics from an in-memory listener-backed ring buffer.
tools.reason: v0.21 build diagnostics ring buffer for Output Log warning/error/fatal events.

since.description: Optional ISO-8601 UTC lower bound; entries with ts >= since are returned.

limit.description: Maximum diagnostics entries to return. Defaults to 200 and is clamped to 1..1000.

tools.notes: In-memory only; the listener stores warning/error/fatal entries in a 5000-entry ring buffer and computes suggested hints at read time.

tools.name: unreal.project_settings_get
tools.title: Get Project Setting
tools.description: Reads a project setting; pass `effective=true` to get the current runtime/PIE value when available (else returns the configured default).
tools.reason: v0.15 chunk 1 C++ readback inspector: read-only project settings access closes the get/inspect gap without Python.

key.description: Project settings key or dot-path inside the category, for example DefaultGameMode, DefaultInputAxisMappings, or DefaultRHI.

effective.description: Return the current runtime/PIE value when available; otherwise return the configured default.

defaultValue.description: Configured default value returned by the default read path when effective=true returns a different runtime value.

tools.notes: C++ only. MVP maps common categories to UInputSettings, URendererSettings, and hardcoded config sections, returning KEY_NOT_FOUND for unsupported edge cases.

tools.name: unreal.bp_delete_node
tools.title: Delete Blueprint Node
tools.description: Deletes a user-deletable Blueprint graph node by NodeGuid and reports the severed pin links.
tools.reason: v0.15 chunk 2a C++ Blueprint refactor tool: bounded node deletion by GUID without Python.

nodeGuid.description: NodeGuid as 32 hex digits or hyphenated GUID text.

tools.notes: PIE-blocked. Returns BLUEPRINT_NOT_FOUND, GRAPH_NOT_FOUND, NODE_NOT_FOUND, or NODE_NOT_DELETABLE on structured errors.

tools.name: unreal.bp_delete_variable
tools.title: Delete Blueprint Variable
tools.description: Deletes a Blueprint member variable after checking local reference nodes unless force=true.
tools.reason: v0.15 chunk 2a C++ Blueprint refactor tool: bounded variable deletion with reference-count guard.

variableName.description: Blueprint member variable name to delete.

force.description: Delete even when reference nodes are present.

tools.notes: PIE-blocked. force=false returns REFERENCES_PRESENT with referenceCount instead of mutating when references exist.

tools.name: unreal.bp_delete_function
tools.title: Delete Blueprint Function
tools.description: Deletes a user function graph and reports caller nodes found in the same Blueprint.
tools.reason: v0.15 chunk 2a C++ Blueprint refactor tool: bounded user function graph deletion without Python.

functionName.description: User function graph name to delete.

tools.notes: PIE-blocked. Built-in graphs such as EventGraph and UserConstructionScript return FUNCTION_NOT_DELETABLE.

tools.name: unreal.bp_rename_variable
tools.title: Rename Blueprint Variable
tools.description: Renames a Blueprint member variable and rewrites local graph references.
tools.reason: v0.15 chunk 2a C++ Blueprint refactor tool: variable rename plus local graph reference rewrite without Python.

newName.description: New valid Blueprint identifier for the member variable.

tools.notes: PIE-blocked. Invalid identifiers return INVALID_NAME with reason not-an-identifier or reserved-word; existing variables return RENAME_COLLISION.

tools.name: unreal.bp_rename_function
tools.title: Rename Blueprint Function
tools.description: Renames a user function graph and rewrites local caller nodes.
tools.reason: v0.15 chunk 2a C++ Blueprint refactor tool: function graph rename plus local caller rewrite without Python.

newName.description: New valid Blueprint identifier for the function graph.

tools.notes: PIE-blocked. Built-in graph renames return FUNCTION_NOT_RENAMABLE; name collisions with graphs or functions return RENAME_COLLISION.

tools.name: unreal.bp_add_macro_graph
tools.title: Add Blueprint Macro Graph
tools.description: Creates a user macro graph on a Blueprint after fixed-name validation and collision checks.
tools.reason: v0.15 chunk 2b C++ Blueprint refactor tool: macro graph creation with identifier validation and collision checks.

macroName.description: New valid Blueprint macro graph name.

tools.notes: PIE-blocked. Invalid identifiers return INVALID_NAME with reason not-an-identifier or reserved-word; existing graph/function/macro/variable names return RENAME_COLLISION.

tools.name: unreal.bp_delete_macro_graph
tools.title: Delete Blueprint Macro Graph
tools.description: Deletes a Blueprint macro graph after checking local macro instance references unless force=true.
tools.reason: v0.15 chunk 2b C++ Blueprint refactor tool: guarded macro graph deletion with reference-count reporting.

macroName.description: Existing Blueprint macro graph name to delete.

force.description: Delete even when UK2Node_MacroInstance references are present.

tools.notes: PIE-blocked. Missing macros return MACRO_NOT_FOUND; force=false returns REFERENCES_PRESENT with referenceCount when macro instance nodes still reference the graph.

tools.name: unreal.bp_interface_add
tools.title: Add Blueprint Interface
tools.description: Adds a Blueprint-implementable interface to a Blueprint and conforms exposed interface function graphs.
tools.reason: v0.15 chunk 2b C++ Blueprint refactor tool: interface implementation with Blueprint usability checks.

interfacePath.description: Full UInterface-derived class path to implement.

tools.notes: PIE-blocked. Missing class paths return INTERFACE_NOT_FOUND; non-interface, Blueprint-unusable, prohibited, or already implemented interfaces return INTERFACE_NOT_USABLE.

tools.name: unreal.bp_interface_remove
tools.title: Remove Blueprint Interface
tools.description: Removes an implemented interface from a Blueprint and optionally preserves its function graphs.
tools.reason: v0.15 chunk 2b C++ Blueprint refactor tool: interface removal with optional function preservation.

interfacePath.description: Full implemented interface class path to remove.

preserveFunctions.description: Keep interface function graphs as orphan functions instead of deleting them.

tools.notes: PIE-blocked. Missing Blueprint assets return BLUEPRINT_NOT_FOUND; absent or non-interface implementations return INTERFACE_NOT_FOUND.

tools.name: unreal.asset_move
tools.title: Move Asset
tools.description: Moves or renames one asset to a new /Game package path using Unreal AssetTools, with optional dry-run planning.
tools.reason: v0.15 chunk 5 migration tool: single-asset move/rename through AssetTools with dry-run planning.
tools.notes: PIE-blocked. Dry run reports referencer count and destination collision. Real run uses IAssetTools::RenameAssets and can optionally fix old-path redirectors.

tools.name: unreal.redirector_fixup
tools.title: Fix Up Redirectors
tools.description: Scans a /Game subtree for UObjectRedirector assets and runs AssetTools fixup to rewrite referencers and remove fixed redirectors.
tools.reason: v0.15 chunk 5 migration tool: Content Browser redirector cleanup through AssetTools fixup.
tools.notes: PIE-blocked. Default dryRun=true. Real run reports remaining redirectors as failures and supports failOnAnyError.

tools.name: unreal.dependency_remap
tools.title: Remap Asset Dependencies
tools.description: Replaces references to one asset with references to another asset using ObjectTools consolidation without deleting the source by default.
tools.reason: v0.15 chunk 5 migration tool: replace references to one asset with another using ObjectTools consolidation.
tools.notes: PIE-blocked. Default dryRun=true. Source deletion is opt-in with deleteSourceAfter=true.

tools.name: unreal.project_version_migration
tools.title: Project Version Migration
tools.description: Updates a .uproject EngineAssociation between UE 5.6 and UE 5.7 and reports remaining manual rebuild steps.
tools.reason: v0.15 chunk 5 migration tool: reversible .uproject EngineAssociation edit between UE 5.6 and UE 5.7.
tools.notes: PIE-blocked. Default dryRun=true. Does not cook, regenerate project files, run UnrealVersionSelector, or rebuild binaries.

tools.name: unreal.activity_log_annotate
tools.title: Annotate Activity Log
tools.description: Writes a user_intent or ai_summary ActivityLog event for Task Atlas extraction and refreshes local task JSON files.
tools.reason: v0.17 Task Atlas annotation tool for user intent and assistant completion summaries.
tools.notes: v0.17 only writes frozen ActivityLog annotations and refreshes local Saved/UnrealMcp/Tasks JSON. It does not call an LLM.

tools.name: unreal.task_list
tools.title: List Task Atlas Tasks
tools.description: Refreshes Task Atlas JSON files from ActivityLog and lists extracted tasks with rating, pin state, and critical path.
tools.reason: v0.17 Task Atlas read-only task listing with optional rating/pin filters.
tools.notes: Read-only from MCP policy perspective, but it may refresh derived task JSON from ActivityLog while preserving rating and pin choices.

tools.name: unreal.task_describe
tools.title: Describe Task Atlas Task
tools.description: Returns the full persisted Task Atlas JSON document plus task path/status fields for one taskId.
tools.reason: v0.17 Task Atlas read-only task detail lookup.
tools.notes: Rejects unsafe taskId strings before resolving the Saved/UnrealMcp/Tasks JSON path.

tools.name: unreal.task_rate
tools.title: Rate Task Atlas Task
tools.description: Persists a success, failed, or unrated rating for an existing Task Atlas task and appends a task_rating ActivityLog event.
tools.reason: v0.17 Task Atlas user rating tool with local JSON persistence.
tools.notes: The rating event is a local audit annotation. It does not promote the task to skills or RAG.

tools.name: unreal.task_pin
tools.title: Pin Task Atlas Task
tools.description: Persists a pinned state for an existing Task Atlas task and appends a task_pin_change ActivityLog event with a critical path snapshot.
tools.reason: v0.17 Task Atlas pinning tool for workflow curation.
tools.summaryTemplate: Changed Task Atlas pin state for {args.taskId}
tools.notes: Pinning is functional in v0.17 and remains a user lock that retrospective label backfill never overwrites.

tools.name: unreal.task_label_backfill
tools.title: Backfill Task Atlas Labels
tools.description: Uses the configured Anthropic Messages provider to infer short labels for unpinned Task Atlas tasks that still have Session timestamp placeholders.
tools.reason: v0.19 Task Atlas retrospective labeling for placeholder workflow labels with pinned/user-edited safeguards.
tools.notes: Calls Anthropic through configured provider settings, preferring claude-haiku-4-5 and falling back to claude-sonnet-4-6. Missing provider config returns success with no_provider_configured skips. The tool never overwrites pinned tasks or labels outside the exact Session YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM placeholder with empty userIntentText.
